title: Trim standalone player state-builder allocation churn
summary: 0.6.3 keeps the clean 0.6.0 baseline and reuses a scratch list when building standalone player state JSON so the player action and control-surface state lane does less transient allocation work.
reasoning: The current player direction is performance only. After 0.6.2 removed redundant hot-tick audio reapply, the next safe bounded trim is the standalone state-builder path that gets exercised across load, transport, control-surface, preset, and audio receipts. Reusing the selection list here cuts allocation churn without changing playback, joystick routing, or sync behavior.

changes.summary: Reuses a scratch list in `BuildStandalonePlayerSelectedStateJson()` instead of allocating a fresh `List<StandalonePlayerRecord>` on every state build.
changes.reasoning: This keeps the performance slice behavioraly neutral while reducing garbage in a frequently used state and receipt path.

verification: Stamp version 0.6.3 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build the player release from the 0.6.x baseline and confirm release validation passes.
Verify the runtime still compiles cleanly after the state-builder maintenance change.
knownIssues: This slice is intentionally narrow and does not change audio sync, video sync, or any joystick behavior.
The future dev package should include a packaged scene, but that packaging policy update is kept out of this performance-maintenance slice.
